metadata.description: Academic known-values for technical indicator validation
metadata.note: Indicators use pandas EWM (Wilder smoothing via alpha=1/period) which differs from textbook SMA-seeded smoothing in early periods but converges after ~50 bars. Reference values are for converged behavior.

rsi.source: Wilder (1978) 'New Concepts in Technical Trading Systems' — RSI definition
rsi.description: All gains, no losses — RSI should be 100

rsi.source: Wilder (1978) — RSI for monotonically decreasing prices
rsi.description: All losses, no gains — RSI should be 0

rsi.source: Wilder (1978) — RSI for alternating gains and losses (equal magnitude)
rsi.description: Equal avg_gain and avg_loss — RSI should be 50
RSI is bounded between 0 and 100 for any valid input

macd.source: Appel (1979) 'The Moving Average Convergence Divergence Trading Method'
macd.description: MACD histogram for constant price series is zero (EMA12 == EMA26)

expected.note: MACD line = EMA12 - EMA26 = 0 for constant price; histogram = 0

macd.source: Appel (1979) — MACD for linearly increasing prices
macd.description: Steadily increasing prices produce positive MACD (fast EMA > slow EMA)

expected.note: Fast EMA responds quicker to uptrend, producing positive MACD line

macd.source: Appel (1979) — MACD for linearly decreasing prices
macd.description: Steadily decreasing prices produce negative MACD

expected.note: Fast EMA drops quicker, producing negative MACD line

bollinger_bands.source: Bollinger (2001) 'Bollinger on Bollinger Bands' — width for constant price
bollinger_bands.description: Constant price has zero standard deviation, so BB width = 0

expected.note: stddev = 0 for constant prices, so upper - lower = 0

bollinger_bands.source: Bollinger (2001) — BB width uses population stddev (ddof=0)
bollinger_bands.description: Known price series with hand-calculable stddev

expected.note: BB width = (upper - lower) / middle = 2 * num_std * std / middle

bollinger_bands.source: Bollinger (2001) — BB width proportional to volatility
bollinger_bands.description: Higher price variance produces wider bands

expected.note: BB width should be larger for more volatile series

adx.source: Wilder (1978) — ADX for strong trend (monotonically increasing)
adx.description: Strong uptrend produces high ADX (above 25 threshold)

expected.note: No clear trend in oscillating market

atr_percent.source: Wilder (1978) — ATR definition: True Range smoothed
atr_percent.description: ATR for constant OHLC equals zero (no range)

expected.note: No price range means TR=0 for every bar

atr_percent.description: True range is max of three non-negative values
Wider high-low ranges produce larger ATR%

williams_r.source: Williams — %R for price at highest high
williams_r.description: When close equals the highest high of the period, %R = 0

williams_r.source: Williams — %R for price at lowest low
williams_r.description: When close equals the lowest low of the period, %R = -100

stochastic_rsi.source: Chande & Kroll (1994) — StochRSI for constant RSI is 50 (zero range)
stochastic_rsi.description: When RSI is flat over the stoch window, StochRSI = 50

rate_of_change.source: StockCharts — ROC for unchanged price is 0
StockCharts — ROC for halved price is -50%
